Nope.
A poster of an attractive dzaan in Utopian Skies-branded VR meta-glasses hangs on the wall here. Garish, eye-catching colors make it hard to ignore - a perfect advertisement.
You already own this decoration! Perhaps you can sell this copy instead...

This poster has been unlocked for decorating! You may hang it via the “Decorate” button in certain locations of your ship, such as your bed, the boarding ramp, the crew security console, or the navigation terminal.
Nope.

A poster of a strong-looking Dzaan with a robotic arm hangs on the wall here. It appears to be an advertisement for the latest cybernetic aug from Dhaal’s Paragon zaibatsu. Their chosen model seems quite backed up as well, butting up against the bounds of what polite Dhaalian society could accept.
You already own this decoration! Perhaps you can sell this copy instead...

This poster has been unlocked for decorating! You may hang it via the “Decorate” button in certain locations of your ship, such as your bed, the boarding ramp, the crew security console, or the navigation terminal.
Nope.

A poster of a curvaceous Dzaan riding a high-pressure milk tank hangs on the wall here. It appears to be an advertisement for the latest synthetic milk drink from Dhaal’s Bright Water zaibatsu, now with 110% more additives! The model looks halfway drunk on milk herself, barely holding on.
Yoink!
Yoink!
Yoink!
A propaganda poster featuring a zaika riding a tank full of milk has been hung up here. You could swipe it. Nobody would care.
A propaganda poster featuring an attractive zaika in a pair of VR-glasses is hanging here. You could swipe it. Nobody would care.

One of the city’s vast, pneumatic trash disposal pipes ruptured just to the ".concat(e,", and the evidence is everywhere. Rotten food piles in the crevices. The air reeks worse than a garbage dump. To the ").concat(e,", the wall of piled detritus prevents any form of land-bound travel. The owners of several franchised garbage trucks appear to be in an argument with a pair of mechanics in Bright Water jumpsuits about who has jurisdiction over the mess - and the payout.")

This section of the street has been overwhelmed by little food carts, advertising their specialties in bright, garish colors. Throbbing neon lanterns have been strung overhead, from one building to the next, lighting up the corridor with a festive appearance. The customers here are as likely to be natives or rushers as the gabilani themselves.\n\nThe busiest booth seems to be a family-run business titled “Bun in the Oven.” Similar-looking gobs scurry from customer to customer, taking orders or delivering large, plump, milk-bread spheres stuffed with various fillings. At the center of the scramble stands a plentifully pregnant gabilani matriarch, cooking each order in a custom-built, portable bakery. Considering the size of her barely apron-covered breasts, you can guess where she gets the milk from.

As the food is going out, the fertilized broodmother catches a glance at your own profile. She offers you a big, sympathetic grin before one of her offspring drops a delivery, turning her attention back to the business at hand. She chucks a plastic spoon at him with pin-point precision and begins re-making the customer’s order. Never a calm moment for the materfamilias!

As you’re taking in the sights and smells of the cookshack, you notice the cook herself taking an eyeful of you. She flicks the big, jingly jewelry hanging from her ears and flutters her dusky eyelids in your direction. If she weren’t already fit to burst, you don’t doubt the amorous matron would be eager to sample what you’ve got to offer!
The road goes east, deeper into the Short Stacks’s Red Light district, or west, to the noisy bazaar.

Your codex beeps. Checking it, you realize you’ve just about reached the coordinates from the strange email you received. Adjusting your position slightly, you find the exact point seems to be a run-down building. It looks vaguely industrial, and quite abandoned. Who could possibly be asking for your help here?
Enter

original: The elevator here is well maintained and discrete enough not to draw any notice. The bottom level of the warehouse, however, is somewhat less so. Massive crates of wood, plastic, and steel fill the space in towering rows that zig, zag, and turn what should be a straight path into a labyrinthine affair. Lucky for you: the stacked freight blocks any line of sight the guards down here might otherwise draw on their unexpected guests.\n\n\t\tWith little immediate risk of ambush, you take a careful assessment of your surroundings. The room is dimly lit by flickering lights high above. Zaika need very little light to operate at peak alertness, but their corpo goons aren’t quite as nocturnal, and the glare of additional floodlights to the east tell you that’s where the kaithrit have gone off to. There’s no evidence of armaments at this end of the warehouse; just a ton of Utopian Skies miscellany. You see 100-gallon jugs of flavored lube, cartons of tail-length condoms, and chests full of body oil promising a long-lasting glisten for stripper poles and strippers alike. Presumably, excess materials for the Zaibatsu-run brothels.

"+(0,iF.zI)(UP||(UP=(0,Bt.Z)(["[var0 |The relative darkness highlights one more important detail: the baleful red power lights on a few dozen security drones mounted high above you! If you head east to the weapons now, you’re sure to be spotted and the kaithrit will be ready for you. However, there’s a ladder near the south wall that leads up to a catwalk... perhaps you could avoid the sentry-bots by taking the high road? |[flags.MALAI_QUEST_COMPONENTS_ACQUIRED |The jig’s up on your intrusion, so there’s no use in climbing the ladder up. You’ve got to get the weapons for Malai and get out now. If you hang around for too long, you’ll just be asking for some Zaibatsu goons to show up and make the mission even more difficult! |The tell-tale red lights on the camera-drones have all winked out, leaving the eastern approach completely surveillance-free. Now you can get the drop on those oblivious cat boys! ] ]"]))),[function(){return!window.malaiQuestSecurityDisabled()}]
Red lights from security camera-drones surveil the eastern approach, making entrance impossible without getting caught.

"+(0,iF.zI)(ZP||(ZP=(0,Bt.Z)(["The elevator north sits, waiting for the next trip back up. The east leads deeper into the warehouse itself. [flags.MALAI_QUEST_COMPONENTS_ACQUIRED||The ascent to the catwalk is against the far southern wall.]"])))
Next
Security
Security

You enter the building, keeping your [pc.weaponMain] at the ready. The entrance is rundown and derelict, but you slip inside without issue. The inside is dark, but there is enough light to see, overhead lights providing some illumination. \n\nSeems the place might not be so abandoned after all.
Forward

You stand in what is surely a loading - or perhaps unloading - bay area. Large roll-up doors sit on the left wall, while towards the right side of the room lie smashed and poorly stacked pallets are littered about. A rather strange-looking piece of machinery sits nearby: some sort of Dhaalian version of a forklift you wager. Whatever it is, it looks completely and utterly ruined, much like the rest of the building.\n\nDespite the state of the structure, the doors are solid and sealed tight. Nobody is getting in or out through them.

Looking around, you find yourself surrounded by a sea of vending machines.

At first, you think the factory must have produced them, but on closer inspection you realize they seem much too new to be produced here, although they are only marginally less destroyed.

Smashed, vandalized, or otherwise scarred, the machines look like they’ve taken a considerable amount of abuse, although they seem relatively new to your eye at least. Regarding them closer, you notice a common trend among all the machines: they dispense one sort of drug or another.

You walk down a darkened hallway, the lights on the ceiling blinking worse than anywhere you’ve been so far. Instinctively, you find your guard raising, wondering what might be lurking in the shadows.

You find yourself in a concourse. The nexus of halls around you is better lit than most of the structure, but only just so. Each hallway is dark and foreboding in its own way, daring you to enter them at your own peril.

This appears to be the largest hallway, easily wide enough for five people to walk abreast. You can’t help but feel as if you’re walking into a cavern moving through it. The dust and debris littered along its length feels heavier than the rest of the building, the western wing is clearly less traveled than the rest of the derelict structure.

The biggest room in the whole facility, you wander around the massive production floor of the abandoned factory. Machinery, coated with dust and shattered ceiling tiles, runs wall-to-wall up the length of the cavernous chamber. You can’t even begin to guess what the factory may have once produced; all the machinery is foreign to you, and not a trace of product has been left behind. What you can tell is almost nothing in the room looks operable - except for ".concat(flags.NARC_DUNG_CRANE>=2?"":"maybe ","one thing.

Looking up towards the ceiling, a massive crane looms overhead, ").concat(2===flags.NARC_DUNG_CRANE?"holding aloft the heavy crate with only a small amount of strain, revealing a previously hidden doorway.":"connected to a massive crate resting on the northern wall of the room. There seems to be something behind the huge metal box, but you can’t quite tell what.","

To the south, a rusted and worn set of stairs leads upwards towards ").concat(null!==(e=getCurrentRoom().south)&&void 0!==e&&e.isExplored?"the crane’s controls.":"some sort of landing.")

Entering the unsealed room, you find yourself in what feels like a tomb. The very air itself tastes like death on your tongue, stale and dust-laden, scratching its way down the back of your throat. A blanket of ashen grime is painted thick onto every surface, stray particles drifting as you make your way inside.

The room is small, and feels confining rather than cozy. The far wall is covered with a mountain of monitors, most of them cracked or otherwise destroyed. Below them sits a daunting console, likely to control the many screens above. It seems completely non functional, but you wonder if power could be rerouted again. You dismiss the notion after a moment, noticing several wires at its base chewed to shreds by some local animal.

The purpose of the room isn’t apparent to you until you look leftward, spotting a few hooks on the wall. An old jacket, rotten and falling apart, hangs from one of the hooks; the scrawling across the back of it is impossible to read for quite a few reasons. ".concat(flags.NARC_DUNG_SECURITY_KEY?"":"On the hook next to the jacket, a heavy looking belt hangs, a shabby-looking, hardlight baton and an ancient-looking flashlight hanging off of it.","

You’re standing inside of an old security station.")
Inspect Belt

original: Making your way to the top of the deathtrap that was once a stairwell, you find yourself overlooking the factory floor, and faced with a vintage control board. Even someone not familiar with heavy machinery could parse that the controls are for the overhead crane,
which are now lit up and seemingly ready to use.
but they are dead once more.
but they don’t seem to have any power.
Use controls
Attempt to control the crane.
The controls are dead.

The eastern hall of the building seems cleaner than the rest of the structure, although not by much. The floor at least shows evidence of many trips down it - quite recently, as well. You also notice pronounced score marks on the tiles, as if something heavy was dragged along it. Examining the patterns, you start to think it might have been many heavy somethings...

You find yourself in front of an open panel on a wall, staring at a mix of old and new tech. The panel is clearly a power relay of some kind, and it looks like somebody has done extensive work on it. Unlike the rest of the building, everything is fully functional, with enough juice passing through the relay to power the whole structure. From what you can tell, it looks like it’s been modified to reroute to another location...

".concat(flags.NARC_DUNG_CRANE?"You think you’ve rerouted all the power you can safely manage.":"If you tinker with it, you might be able to reroute some power back in the western wing...","
")
Reroute
Attempt to reroute power.
You’ve done all you can do.

The northern hall of the factory feels brighter than the others, the lights on the ceiling shine with noticeably stronger intensity. You can’t help but wonder if that’s incidental, or if somebody has repaired the light fixtures lately? The added light makes watching your step easier, so you can focus on watching your back.

You find yourself in a very different environment from the rest of the factory. What was once clearly office space for the building is now a near-incomprehensible mess. Desks, chairs, and tables are spread around the area, most of them upended and otherwise trashed. While the rest of the building has a distinctly abandoned and forgotten feeling, the space here feels different to you. It almost seems like it was ransacked; and based on the way the dust in the room is disturbed, it must have happened recently. \n\nTo the west, ".concat(null!==(e=getCurrentRoom().west)&&void 0!==e&&e.isExplored?"a door leads to a small infirmary.":"you see a plain door."," On the other side of the room, a much more ornate door stands proud against the ruin of the room.")
With your security card, maybe now you can get inside...
It leads to the manager’s office, repurposed for Jezzy’s personal use.
Checking the door, you find it locked. You’ll need some sort of key to enter...

What was once surely a proud office has been reduced to a den of depravity.

The very air is thick with sin, tasting of smoke, sweat, and spunk. Nearly every surface in the room is loaded up with empty bottles, food containers, or some sort of illicit substance, many of which you don’t recognize. Even the floor is covered in a thick layer of junk, some of it trash, but you manage to pick out a few oddities among the rubbish. Jezzy must have been squatting in here for quite some time, and seems to not be the sort to tidy up after herself.

A large couch sits in the middle of the room, visually sticking out like a sore thumb from the rest of the decor. The office’s desk has been shoved against a wall facing the couch, a very modern monitor planted on top of it, playing some sort of documentary about deep sea cephalopods. 

".concat(-1===flags.NARC_DUNG_JEZZY?"Jezzy is where you left her, laying nearly comatose on the couch. The slight rise and fall of her ample chest tells you she’s breathing, but her glassed-over expression makes you feel more than a little uncomfortable.":"","

Next to the couch, you spot a compact portable computer on the floor").concat(flags.NARC_DUNG_VAGINA_DOCKED?" plugged into what looks like... a vagina?":".")
Computer
Computer
Check what’s on the computer.

You find yourself in what was once a small infirmary, but are not so sure the label still applies.

The shelves are bare: not even a single bandaid to be found among them. Unlike the offices however, this space doesn’t feel like it was ransacked - it just feels empty. Medical diagrams line the walls, seemingly perfectly preserved, while a desk and chair towards the side are both upright and intact. An examination table on the far side of the room does look like it’s seen better days, but the wear seems to be due to age rather than abuse.

As you take in your surroundings, something about the room really starts to stand out: there is almost no dust. While the rest of the building is coated in the stuff, the infirmary is pristine. The reason why seems obvious: somebody has been cleaning it.

Despite the fact that it’s about as far from stocked as imaginable, nothing is out of place. Even the items on the desk appear neatly arranged.
Desk
Search the desk.
You already took the vaginal unit from the desk.

Clean and sterile, the basement of the factory is nothing like the structure’s main floor. The floors, walls, and ceiling are all pristine white, enameled and gleaming. The lights on the ceiling are soft but effective and fully functional, unlike the fixtures up above. 

You sincerely doubt this was the original look of the basement; its getting the impression it was painstakingly renovated to its current state. The cost of which would have been sizable you reason, considering the difference. The derelict factory above must surely be a front in order to hide whatever is going on down here.

The only way forward is through a doorway to the north side of the chamber.

You find yourself standing inside of what appears to be a super lab.

Cutting edge tech lines the walls, much of it unrecognizable to you. Some of it however, you are at least familiar with, either directly, or from things you’ve seen. Most of it is medical, including a rather impressive surgical bed that even the most affluent hospital would feel the sting of purchasing. Most of the tech seems more related to chemistry however, with one station in particular appearing to have quite a bit of research material on it. You also notice a considerable amount of tech related to automatons, both for maintenance and construction, as well as what seems to be a series of charging stations. It doesn’t take a genius".concat(pc.isBimbo()||pc.isBro()?", thankfully,":""," to realize this is where the nursedroids recharge.

What makes it very obvious is a table next to the station, the disassembled lower half of a nursedroid laid out on top of it in a mess of parts.").concat(flags.NARC_DUNG_VAG3?"":" Your throat dries at the sight, and you grip your [pc.weaponMain] tighter. Are you too late?","

To the north, another door sits ominously.")
